啊，科学，不知疲倦地努力回答鳄鱼呼吸富含氦的空气时的声音，冰冻粪便制成的刀是否构成可行的切割工具等紧迫的问题。这些和其他不同寻常的研究课题今晚在一个虚拟的仪式上获得了荣誉-这要归功于正在进行的大流行-宣布2020年年度搞笑诺贝尔奖的获奖者。你可以在上面观看颁奖典礼的实况转播。
搞笑诺贝尔奖成立于1991年，是对诺贝尔奖的善意恶搞，诺贝尔奖表彰的成就首先让人发笑，然后让人思考。这个毫无歉意的装腔作势的颁奖典礼通常包括迷你歌剧、科学演示和全天候演讲，专家们必须在24秒内解释两次，第二次只用七个字。获奖感言以60秒为限。正如格言所暗示的那样，被授予荣誉的研究乍一看可能看起来很荒谬，但这并不意味着它没有科学价值。传统上，获奖者还会在颁奖典礼后的第二天在波士顿进行公开演讲；今年，演讲将在几周后以网络直播的形式进行。
获奖者将获得永恒的搞笑诺贝尔奖声誉和一张来自津巴布韦的10万亿美元钞票。这是一个长期的搞笑诺贝尔奖恶作剧。由于通货膨胀和恶性通货膨胀，津巴布韦在2009年停止使用本国货币；在其最低点，100万亿美元的钞票大约相当于40美分美国。(去年，津巴布韦储备银行推出了Zollar，作为潜在的替代品。)。2009年搞笑诺贝尔数学奖被授予时任RBZ负责人吉迪恩·戈诺(Gideon Gono)，表彰他为人们提供了一种简单、日常的方式来应对范围很广的数字-从非常小到非常大-让他的银行打印面额从1美分(0.01美元)到100万亿美元(100亿美元)的钞票。
引证：斯蒂芬·雷伯(Stephan Reber)、西村武(Takeshi Nishimura)、朱迪思·贾尼施(Judith Janisch)、马克·罗伯逊(Mark Robertson)和特库姆塞·费奇(Tecumseh Fitch)，因为他们诱导一只雌性中国鳄鱼在密闭的房间里咆哮，房间里充满了富含氦的空气。
鳄鱼、短吻鳄和类似的非鸟类爬行动物声音极大，容易大声吼叫，特别是在交配季节。获奖者很好奇，这些发声是否可能是一种宣传体型的手段(已经表明，女性更喜欢与比自己块头大的男性交配)。为了验证这一假设，研究人员在佛罗里达州圣奥古斯丁扬子鳄农场动物园招募了一只成年中国扬子鳄，它在接受医疗程序后被隔离在一个长方形的塑料浴缸里。众所周知，这只鳄鱼经常吼叫，通常是对附近围栏里的40只美国扬子鳄的咆哮做出反应。因此，研究人员能够在两种情况下，通过回放记录的风箱，让她在提示下咆哮：呼吸正常的空气，或者空气中混合着氦气。
这样做的目的是不让可怜的鳄鱼表演流行的聚会戏法。作者解释说，这是观察这些生物是否表现出声道共振(技术上称为共振峰频率)的好方法，哺乳动物和鸟类将其用作身体大小的指示器。事实上，作者确实得出结论，他们的雌性扬子鳄有声道共振的证据。此外，由于鸟类和鳄鱼与所有恐龙有着共同的祖先，更好地了解它们的发声系统也可能为研究灭绝的始祖龙的交流提供洞察力，作者们在2015年的论文中写道。
引文：米兰达·贾科明(Miranda Giacomin)和尼古拉斯·鲁尔(Nicholas Rule)，因为他们设计了一种通过检查眉毛来识别自恋者的方法。
在心理学家中，浮夸的自恋是一种阴暗的人格特征，其特征是自私、自私、理所当然和虚荣。尽管这些人通常表面上很有魅力，但有些人几乎第一眼就能看到自恋者--这是一种宝贵的社交技能，能让他们避免落入自恋者的毒网。贾科明和鲁尔想要查明这项技能背后的机制。此前的研究表明，当我们遇到新朋友时，首先会注意到的是一个人的脸，所以他们招募了39名本科生，让他们摆出中性表情的姿势拍照，然后让他们填写自恋人格表。
贾科明和鲁尔随后使用这些照片进行了一系列研究，要求参与者根据他们认为自己有多自恋来对每一张脸进行评级。眉毛是脸部最有表现力的特征之一，研究人员发现，人们依靠眉毛准确地辨别出浮夸的自恋者-特别是根据他们的眉毛的与众不同。我想这里的教训是要当心那些眉毛独特、修剪得很好的人。
引用：印度和巴基斯坦政府，因为他们的外交官在半夜偷偷地按对方的门铃，然后在任何人有机会开门之前就跑掉了，这是因为他们的外交官在半夜里偷偷地按对方的门铃，然后在任何人有机会开门之前就跑掉了。
印度和巴基斯坦之间的关系长期以来一直很紧张，但2018年情况变得特别糟糕，仅今年前两个月，克什米尔边境就有434多起违反停火的行为。随着两国关系的进一步恶化，两国外交部似乎也对敌对国家的高级外交官进行了有针对性的骚扰。这包括切断供电和供水，开车尾随外交官，淫秽电话，咄咄逼人的对抗，甚至在凌晨按响外交官的门铃，然后逃跑。我不确定更糟糕的是什么：那些所谓的职业政府代表表现得像小学生，或者一名退休的印度外交官告诉“卫报”，这种骚扰既不新鲜，也不罕见--而且不仅限于印巴关系。
引文：伊万·马克西莫夫和安德烈·波托茨基，他们通过实验确定了当人们高频振动蚯蚓时，活蚯蚓的形状会发生什么。
震动一池水，你会发现，在一个临界频率以上，水面上会形成一种驻波图案。这些都是以迈克尔·法拉第(Michael Faraday)的名字命名的法拉第波，他在19世纪上半叶研究了这种现象。马克西莫夫和波托斯基推断，由于许多活着的有机体大多是由液体组成的，他们认为液体类似于液滴，因此有机体应该在合适的条件下经历驻波。研究人员之所以选择蚯蚓进行实验，是因为它们有一个有弹性的皮肤和充满液体的体腔的静水骨骼。蚯蚓也很便宜，你不需要伦理许可就可以使用它们。这些蠕虫用乙醇固定，然后放在一块薄薄的特氟龙板上，然后垂直振动。研究人员使用激光测振仪检测活体蚯蚓的振动。果不其然，两人记录了向法拉第海浪的关键过渡。
马克西莫夫和波托斯基沿袭了臭名昭著的球形奶牛的传统，将蠕虫的身体建模为充满流体的弹性圆柱壳，用于研究的理论部分。这篇论文还包括了这样一个观察的精髓：也避免了大的振动，因为它们另外会导致粘性液体从蠕虫中喷射出来。我甚至不想知道更多。然而，这个项目并不是为了搞笑而做的。作者争辩说，他们的结果可以用来开发探测和控制生物物理过程(如神经冲动的传播)在活体内的新技术。
引文：克里斯托弗·沃特金斯、胡安·大卫·梁诺梅斯、珍妮·博维、AgnieszkaŻElaźNiewicz、马克斯·科布马赫、马尔科·安东尼奥·科雷拉·瓦雷拉、安娜·玛丽亚·费尔南德斯、丹妮尔·瓦格斯塔夫和萨缪拉·博尔根，因为他们试图量化不同国家之间的国民收入不平等和平均嘴对嘴接吻的数量。
这些获奖者热衷于研究浪漫的嘴对嘴亲吻的文化差异，看看这种行为是否可能是维持长期伴侣关系的一种手段，以及其他好处。因此，他们从世界各地(横跨13个国家和6大洲)招募了3109名参与者进行在线研究。他们发现，在恋爱关系的后期阶段，接吻通常被认为更重要，特别是对年轻的参与者。正如他们假设的那样，在他们的结果中，收入不平等与接吻频率呈正相关。作者总结道，在资源竞争可能更加激烈的国家，个人更多地亲吻伴侣，这可能对在某些类型的恶劣环境中保持长期稳定的伴侣关系起到重要作用。我们将坚持一个由来已久的智慧，那就是接吻仍然是接吻。
引文：&#34；(奚广安)习广安、(莫天祥)莫天祥、(杨康生)杨康生、(杨广生)杨光-生、(凌显四)凌贤思五位中国广西职业杀手，他们以如下方式管理一份热门工作(为钱表演的谋杀)的合同：在接受付款实施谋杀后，奚广安转而将任务转包给莫田祥，后者再将任务转包给杨康生，杨康生再将任务转包给杨光。于是，他把任务转包给了凌宪思，每个后来应征入伍的杀手收取的费用比例都较小，而且没有人真正实施谋杀。&#34；
是的，这真的发生了，而且这一切都是从一场房地产商业纠纷开始的。刺杀目标是一名姓魏的男子，他曾对两家房地产公司提起民事诉讼。这些公司的投资者之一，谭友辉，雇佣了习广安去找人杀魏。莫天祥被许诺200万元，到凌宪思转包的时候，这笔钱已经降到了区区十万元。凌宪思觉得这不值得冒这个险，于是联系了魏。两人在一家咖啡馆相遇，林贤思说服魏摆好姿势拍照，被捆绑并堵住了嘴，然后消失了10天。显然，计划是在不犯罪的情况下收取10万元的赔偿金，但最终整个阴谋被曝光。去年，所有被告都被判处有期徒刑，刑期从三年零六个月(席广安)到两年零七个月(凌宪思)不等。
引文：理查德·维特(Richard Vetter)，因为他收集了证据，证明许多昆虫学家(研究昆虫的科学家)害怕蜘蛛，而蜘蛛不是昆虫。
好吧，让我们公平地说：这很有趣，但维特并不知道其中的区别，因为他在2013年的论文中指出，参与他的研究的41名昆虫学家中，有两名是收集经理，而且无论他们处理的昆虫种类多种多样，他们对蜘蛛的反应仍然不同于昆虫。他后来区分了蜘蛛和节肢动物。只是他发现蜘蛛恐惧症在昆虫学家中的盛行令人惊讶，因为他们与许多非昆虫学家认为同样令人厌恶的生物有着如此密切的合作关系，他想了解更多可能导致这种厌恶的原因。仅就所有丰富多彩的个人细节而言，这项研究就值得一读。我个人最喜欢的是童年时与蜘蛛的负面遭遇：有一个人(从4岁到8岁)反复做噩梦，在她的房子周围跑来跑去，撞上一只人类大小的蜘蛛，然后在被吃掉之前醒来。&#34；我能感受到你，无名的昆虫学家。

根据母板的一份报告，两名美国参议员要求亚马逊停止通过他们的社交媒体帖子监视工人。两周前，另一份母板报告显示，该公司正在监控其员工在美国、英国和西班牙的数十个私人和公共社交媒体群。
参议员谢罗德·布朗(D-OH)和罗恩·怀登(D-OR)周三给亚马逊首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯写了一封信，敦促这家科技巨头结束其反工人、反工会的努力，包括倡导运营社会倾听团队(Advocacy Operations Social Listing Team)。
参议员们联合写道，这种监控的规模、亚马逊不遗余力地向你自己的员工隐瞒它的程度，以及它承认的目的，都非常令人不安，表明亚马逊认为自己的员工构成了多大的威胁。他们呼吁亚马逊将精力集中在改善公司在新冠肺炎疫情期间的健康和安全记录上。
主板最初的报告详细说明了亚马逊是如何使用一种工具在网上找到计划罢工或抗议亚马逊的司机的。在大流行期间，工人们通过举行罢工和抗议活动，加大了对改善工作条件的倡导力度。
在给Business Insider的一份声明中，亚马逊的一位发言人表示：我们有多种方式收集司机的反馈，我们的团队每天都在工作，以确保我们倡导改善司机的体验，特别是通过直接听取司机的意见。接到通知后，我们发现交付团队中有一个组正在从封闭的组中聚合信息。虽然他们试图支持司机，但这种方法不符合我们的标准，他们也不再这样做了，因为我们有其他方式让司机向我们提供反馈。
亚马逊一直是员工组建工会的坚定反对者，以至于亚马逊在9月初发布了一个职位空缺，招聘一名分析师，该分析师将发现员工成立工会的努力，然后向上级汇报。“商业内幕”(Business Insider)的亚伦·福尔摩斯(Aaron Holmes)联系其请其置评后，该公司迅速删除了这份工作清单。
亚马逊在大流行期间获利丰厚，因为呆在家里的订单驱使人们走进自己的家，进入在线市场。首席执行官杰夫·贝佐斯(Jeff Bezos)的巨额财富反过来也出现了飙升-他现在是世界上最富有的人，净资产超过2000亿美元。
在大流行期间，多个行业的业务大幅下滑，零工经济也不例外。活动的下滑加剧了该领域的竞争，以至于亚马逊Flex司机最近被发现将智能手机挂在全食超市(Whole Foods)和快递站外的树上，以求站稳脚跟。离订单发源地很近，这意味着当订单准备交付给接收者时，系统更有可能选择它们。

note_en:
Two US senators have demanded that Amazon stop spying on workers through their social media posts, per a report from  Motherboard. The news comes two weeks after a separate Motherboard  report revealed the company was monitoring dozens of private and public social media groups of its workers in the US, the UK, and Spain.
 Senators Sherrod Brown (D-OH) and Ron Wyden (D-OR) wrote a  letter to Amazon CEO Jeff Bezos on Wednesday urging the tech giant to end its "anti-worker, anti-union efforts, including the Advocacy Operations Social Listening team."
 "The magnitude of this surveillance, the lengths to which Amazon went to keep it hidden from your own workers, and its admitted purpose are extremely disturbing and are indicative of just how much of a threat Amazon perceives its own workers to be," the senators jointly wrote. They called upon Amazon to instead focus their efforts on improving the company's health and safety record during the COVID-19 pandemic.
 Motherboard's initial report detailed how Amazon was using a tool to find drivers online who were planning for a strike or protest against Amazon. Workers had been ramping up their advocacy for better working conditions during the pandemic by staging strikes and protests.
   In a statement to Business Insider, an Amazon spokesperson said: "We have a variety of ways to gather driver feedback and we have teams who work every day to ensure we're advocating to improve the driver experience, particularly through hearing from drivers directly. Upon being notified, we discovered one group within our delivery team that was aggregating information from closed groups. While they were trying to support drivers, that approach doesn't meet our standards, and they are no longer doing this as we have other ways for drivers to give us their feedback."
 Amazon has been a staunch opponent to its workers forming a union, so much so that Amazon  posted a job opening in early September for an analyst that would find employee's efforts to unionize and then report back to higher-ups. It quickly deleted the job listing after Business Insider's Aaron Holmes reached out for comment.
 Amazon has profited heavily during the pandemic as stay-at-home orders have driven people into their homes and onto online marketplaces. CEO Jeff Bezos has in turn seen a spike in his already colossal fortune — he is now the richest person in the world  with a net worth of more than $200 billion.
 Business has plummeted across multiple industries during the pandemic, and the gig economy is no exception. A slump in activity has increased competition in the realm, so much so that Amazon Flex drivers  were recently found to be hanging smartphones in trees located outside of Whole Foods stores and delivery stations for a leg up. The proximity to where the orders originated meant that the system was more likely to choose them when an order was ready to be delivered to its recipient.

note:
消息传出后，这家加速器的创始人--霍华德、格雷泽和首席执行官泰勒·米切尔--与我们一起参加了TechCrunch的Disrupt大会，讨论他们的愿景。格雷泽(与霍华德合作的电影包括“阿波罗13号”(Apollo 13)、“美丽的心灵”(A Beautiful Mind)和即将上映的Netflix的“Hillbilly Elegy”)回忆了25年前的好莱坞，他说那是一个“不透明”的体系，原创编剧经常很难打入这个体系，他觉得这种影响可以“让进入好莱坞的渠道民主化”。
“我们怎样才能创造机会进入好莱坞这个传媒业的就业中心呢？”他说。
首先，米切尔描述了他所说的每周评估2000份脚本提交的可伸缩系统。
他说：“我们能够建立一个系统，既利用技术，也利用专家系统，不仅评估作者，而且评估读者-几乎就像金融分析师一样-并试图在没有统计数据的世界里提出衡量标准。”
米切尔还指出，在Impact的第一批87名作家中，39%是BIPOC，10%是LGBTQ，男性和女性各占一半，代表了11个不同的国家。
他说：“如果你试图找到世界上最有才华的作家，他们看起来就像这个世界。”
霍华德表达了类似的观点，他说，这种多样性源于对“新鲜的新声音”的兴趣，“脑海中没有统计目标或议程-这只是以一种真正诚实的方式发生”。
当被问及他们是否有兴趣从社交媒体中寻找新的人才时，霍华德指出，格雷泽总是鼓励他“了解北方正在发生的事情”(又名。在硅谷)。
“现在我们正处于一个创造性的文艺复兴时期，播客和Instagram…应运而生。“找到了进入叙事中心的方式，”霍华德说。
格雷泽说，他经常特别看YouTube。与此同时，他警告说，为这些在线平台创建内容需要不同于编写电影或电视的技能。
“这并不一定会降低他们成功的可能性，但这是一种不同的艺术形式，”他说。“因为写电视剧或剧本，即使是最伟大的剧作家也做不到这一点--你必须接受训练。”
不过，想象力在Instagram上的故事中至少找到了一个想法，围绕一名演员(格雷泽不愿透露是谁，但很可能是阿诺德·施瓦辛格)和一头名为露露(Lulu)的驴子和一匹名为威士忌(Whiskey)的迷你马制作了一部喜剧节目。显然，这个节目吸引了多个竞标者，至于它将在哪里结束，格雷泽说，“它看起来有点像亚马逊。我明天会告诉你的。“
note_en:
Right after the news broke, the accelerator’s founders — Howard, Grazer and CEO  Tyler Mitchell   — joined us at TechCrunch’s Disrupt conference to discuss their vision. Grazer (whose films with Howard include “Apollo 13,” “A Beautiful Mind” and the upcoming “Hillbilly Elegy” for Netflix) recalled the Hollywood of 25 years ago, which he described as an “opaque” system where original writers often struggled to break in, and he felt that Impact could “democratize access to Hollywood.”
 “How can we create opportunity to have access to epicenter of employment in the media business, which is Hollywood?” he said.
  For starters, Mitchell described what he claimed is a scalable system for evaluating 2,000 script submissions every week.
 “We were able to build a system that leverages both technology as well as expert systems evaluating not just the writers, but the readers — almost like financial analysts — and try to come up with metrics in world where there aren’t stats,” he said.
 Mitchell also noted that in Impact’s first cohort of 87 writers, 39% were BIPOC, 10% were LGBTQ and it was split 50-50 between men and women, with 11 different countries represented.
 “If you try to find the most talented writers in the world, they’re going to look like the world,” he said.
 Howard made a similar point, saying that this diversity results from an interest in “fresh new voices” with “no statistical goals or agendas in mind — it’s just happening in a really honest way.”
  Asked whether they’re interested in finding new talent from social media, Howard pointed to Grazer as the one who’s always encouraging him to “know what’s going on up north” (a.k.a. in Silicon Valley).
 “Right now we’re in a creative renaissance with podcasts and Instagrams … finding their way into the center of the narrative,” Howard said.
 Grazer said he often looks at YouTube in particular. At the same time, he cautioned that creating content for these online platforms requires a different skillset than writing movies or TV.
 “It doesn’t reduce the likelihood of their success necessarily, but it’s a different art form,” he said. “Because writing a teleplay or a screenplay, even the greatest playwrights can’t do that particular thing — you have to be trained.”
 Still, Imagine found at least one idea in an Instagram Story, developing a comedic show around an actor (Grazer didn’t want to say who it was, but it’s  probably Arnold Schwarzenegger) with a donkey named Lulu and a miniature horse named Whiskey. Apparently the show has attracted multiple bidders, and as for where it will end up, Grazer said, “It sort of seems like Amazon. I’ll let you know tomorrow.”
title: 罗恩·霍华德和布莱恩·格雷泽表示，他们的加速器可以帮助好莱坞实现多元化。
title_en: Ron Howard and Brian Grazer say that their accelerator can help diversify Hollywood

note:
Playter在Disrupt2020的虚拟主舞台上接受采访，在那里工作多年后，他最近才从首席运营官晋升为首席运营官，此前长期担任首席执行官和创始人的马克·莱伯特辞职，专注于研发。这是他的Playter自承担新职责以来首次公开发表演讲，显然他对波士顿机器人公司(Boston Robotics)有宏伟的计划。
Spot是一款多功能四足机器人，是著名的Big Dog的远祖，最近的商业化表明，Playter和公司对他们提供的产品有巨大的需求，尽管他们不完全确定需求在哪里。
他承认：“我们不确定垂直领域的确切目标是什么，”客户似乎也不确定，他们总共购买了价值7.5万美元的机器人中的约260台，目前正在积极为该平台开发自己的附加组件和行业专用工具。而价格并没有起到威慑作用，他说：“作为一种工业工具，这实际上是相当实惠的。但我们一直非常积极，花了很多钱试图建立一种负担得起的方式来生产这种产品，我们已经在研究继续降低成本的方法。“。
全球大流行也帮助围绕机器人创造了一种紧迫感，将其作为体力劳动的替代或增强。
Playter说：“人们正在意识到，拥有自己的实体代理，能够远程出席，可能比我们之前想象的更重要。”“我们一直认为机器人能够进入危险的地方，但现在因为COVID，危险被重新定义了一点。这场流行病正在加速人们的紧迫感，我认为，这可能会打开我们将用这项技术探索的各种应用。“。
在COVID特定的应用中，该公司处理了远程监测患者的协作请求，以及使用SPOT通过设施进行气溶胶喷雾的自动消毒。“我不知道未来这是否会是一个大市场，但我们认为当时做出回应很重要，”他说。“部分原因是出于对社区和社会的责任感，我们在这里做的事情是正确的。”
当然，最早成功规模化的应用之一是物流，亚马逊等公司已经将机器人技术作为提高生产率和降低劳动力成本的一种方式。波士顿动力公司准备用一种非常不同的机器人-更确切地说是机器人-来帮助搬运箱子和其他类似箱子的物品，这种方式与目前实用的“自动托盘”方法截然不同。
“我们在物流方面有宏伟的计划，”Playter说。“我们将在未来两年内推出一些令人兴奋的新物流产品。我们现在有客户在做概念验证测试。我们将在2021年宣布一些东西，确切地说我们正在做什么，我们将在2022年推出产品。“
该公司已经推出了Pick，这是一种更传统的固定物品挑选系统，他们正在研发下一版的Handle，这是一种像鸟一样的移动机器人，可以抓起箱子并四处移动，而占用的空间相对较小-不超过一两个人站着。这种机动性使其能够卸下集装箱、卡车和其他受限或不可预测的空间等物品。
在采访期间播放的一段视频(你可以在上面观看)中，Handle还显示了与现成的托盘机器人协同工作的画面，Playter强调了这种合作的必要性，而不仅仅是来自单一创建者的机器人之间的合作。
“我们将提供让机器人协同工作的软件，”他说。“现在，我们不必把它们都创造出来。但最终将需要机器人团队来完成其中的一些任务，我们预计能够与一个不同的舰队合作。“。
这款更友善、更温和、对行业更友好的波士顿动力几乎肯定是软银(Softbank)推动的产物，软银于2018年收购了该公司，但也有一个简单的现实，那就是你不能无偿运营一个世界领先的机器人研发机构。但Playter热衷于指出，这家日本科技巨头明白，“我们之所以能处于现在的地位，是因为我们在过去20年里所做的工作，开发了这些先进的能力，所以我们必须继续这样做。”
你不太可能在短期内看到阿特拉斯做真正的工作，那就是阿特拉斯，该公司令人惊讶的敏捷的类人机器人。它只是现在还不实用，而是作为一种声望工程，迫使公司不断向上调整目光。
“这是一个如此复杂的机器人，它能做的事情如此之多，迫使我们创造否则不会创造的工具。人们喜欢它--它是有抱负的，它能吸引人才。“Playter说。
他本人也不例外。他曾经是一名体操运动员，他回忆起看着阿特拉斯跳来跳去的“怀旧时刻”。“公司里的很多人，包括马克，都从人和动物的运动表现中获得灵感，”Playter说。“这种DNA深深植根于我们公司。”
note_en:
Playter, interviewed on the virtual main stage of Disrupt 2020, only recently ascended from COO to that role after many years of working there,  after longtime CEO and founder Marc Raibert stepped aside to focus on R&D. This is his Playter’s first public speaking engagement since taking on the new responsibility, and it’s clear he has big plans for Boston Robotics.
 The recent commercialization of Spot, the versatile quadrupedal robot that is a distant descendant of the famous Big Dog, showed Playter and the company that there is a huge demand for what they’re offering, even if they’re not completely sure where that demand is.
  “We weren’t sure exactly what the target verticals would be,” he admitted, and seemingly neither did the customers, who have collectively bought about 260 of the $75,000 robots and are now actively building their own add-ons and industry-specific tools for the platform. And the price hasn’t been a deterrent, he said: “As an industrial tool this is actually quite affordable. But we’ve been very aggressive, spending a lot of money to try to build an affordable way to produce this, and we’re already working on ways to continue to reduce costs.”
  The global pandemic has also helped create a sense of urgency around robots as an alternative to or augmentation of manual labor.
 “People are realizing that having a physical proxy for themselves, to be able to be present remotely, might be more important than we imagined before,” Playter said. “We’ve always thought of robots as being able to go into dangerous places, but now danger has been redefined a little bit because of COVID. The pandemic is accelerating the sense of urgency and, I think, probably opening up the kinds of applications that we will explore with this technology.”
 Among the COVID-specific applications, the company has fielded requests for collaboration on remote monitoring of patients, and automatic disinfection using spot to carry aerosol spray through a facility. “I don’t know whether that’ll be a big market going forward, but we thought it was important to respond at the time,” he said. “Partly out of a sense of obligation to the community and society that we do the right thing here.”
  One of the earliest applications to scale successfully was, of course, logistics, where companies like Amazon have embraced robotics as a way to increase productivity and lower labor costs. Boston Dynamics is poised to jump into the market with a very different robot — or rather robots — meant to help move boxes and other box-like items around in a very different way from the currently practical “autonomous pallet” method.
 “We have big plans in logistics,” Playter said. “we’re going to have some exciting new logistics products coming out in the next two years. We have customers now doing proof of concept tests. We’ll announce something in 2021, exactly what we’re doing, and we’ll have product available in 2022.”
  The company already offers Pick, a more traditional, stationary item-picking system, and they’re working on the next version of Handle, a birdlike mobile robot that can grab boxes and move them around while taking up comparatively little space — no more than a person or two standing up. This mobility allows it to unload things like shipping containers, trucks, and other confined or less predictable spaces.
  In a video shown during the interview (which you can watch above), Handle is also shown  working in concert with an off-the-shelf pallet robot, and Playter emphasized the need for this kind of cooperation, and not just between robots from a single creator.
 “We’ll be offering software that lets robots work together,” he said. “Now, we don’t have to create them all. But ultimately it will take teams of robots to do some of these tasks, and we anticipate being able to work with a heterogeneous fleet.”
 This kinder, gentler, more industry-friendly Boston Dynamics is almost certainly a product of nudging from Softbank, which acquired the company in 2018, but also the simple reality that you can’t run a world-leading robotics R&D outfit for nothing. But Playter was keen to note that the Japanese tech giant understands that “we’re only in the position we’re in now because of the previous work we’ve done in the last two decades, developing these advanced capabilities, so we have to keep doing that.”
 One thing you won’t likely seeing doing real work any time soon is Atlas, the company’s astonishingly agile humanoid robot. It’s just not practical for anything just yet, but instead acts as a kind of prestige project, forcing the company to constantly adjust its sights upward.
  “It’s such a complex robot, and it can do so much it forces us to create tools we would not otherwise. And people love it — it’s aspirational, it attracts talent,” said Playter.
 And he himself is no exception. Once a gymnast, he recalled “a nostalgic moment” watching Atlas vault around. “A lot of the people in the company, including Marc, have inspiration from the athletic performance of people and animals,” Playter said. “That DNA is deeply embedded in our company.”

骄傲的男孩和其他极右翼团体的成员本周在利用Facebook为即将在俄勒冈州波特兰举行的一场集会宣传和筹集资金，尽管这家社交媒体巨头最近保证，这些极端组织将无法在其平台上组织，而且在经历了一个与该公司有关的政治暴力事件破坏了一个夏天之后。
周三，Facebook的多个页面活跃起来，显示了隶属于骄傲男孩或其他极右翼团体的人，宣传定于9月9日举行的反对“反法”和“黑人生活也是命”的集会。26在波特兰。
这些Facebook页面的链接是由监督组织科技透明度项目(Tech Transparent Project)提供给赫芬顿邮报的。这些链接显示了极右翼团体发布宣传即将到来的集会的视频，并包括一个利用Facebook自己的筹款工具为活动筹集资金的页面。
赫芬顿邮报在周三晚上向Facebook询问了这些页面，到周四下午早些时候，所有页面都已被删除。
Facebook的一位发言人说：“Facebook禁止”骄傲的男孩“和宣传他们的活动。”“出于这个原因，我们已经删除了与即将到来的集会相关的活动、页面和账户，我们破坏这些努力的工作正在进行中。”
但TTP负责人凯蒂·保罗(Katie Paul)表示，Facebook的回应只是该公司在打击其平台上的极端主义方面做得太少、做得太晚的又一个例子，只是在记者提醒后才删除有害内容。
保罗在接受“赫芬顿邮报”采访时表示：“Facebook将更多精力放在清理悲剧事件后的公关上，而不是首先解决其平台是如何为这些事件提供便利的。”
在Facebook周四删除的页面中，有一个名为“骄傲的安全”，上面充满了骄傲男孩的符号和图像。骄傲男孩是一个支持特朗普的新法西斯主义街头帮派，因在全国各地的政治集会上攻击左翼分子而臭名昭著，通常是在波特兰。
9月9日。8日，“自豪的安全”页面通过发布波特兰最近反种族主义抗议活动的视频蒙太奇，并恳求来自全国各地的“爱国者”来波特兰“表明立场”，宣传即将举行的“骄傲的男孩”集会。
周四被移除的另外两页属于极右翼组织，即俄勒冈州爱国者联盟和华盛顿爱国者联盟。这两个团体都发布了一段视频蒙太奇，本质上是骄傲的男孩暴动色情，邀请支持者在9月9日在波特兰“粉碎反法”。26点上涨。
骄傲的安全页面-由一个名叫丹尼尔·图兹(Daniel Tooze)的人运营-利用Facebook自己的筹款工具为这次活动筹集资金。截至周三晚上，已为1万美元的目标筹集了近2000美元。
该页面也在周四被Facebook删除。Facebook的规定表明，该公司从其平台上的此类筹款活动中收取费用。该公司没有回应赫芬顿邮报的一个问题，即它是否从骄傲的男孩募捐活动中收取了费用。
周三，多位名人参加了一场广为宣传的对Facebook旗下Instagram的“止恨谋利”抵制活动。这是一个社会正义团体联盟长达数月的运动中的最新行动，目的是向这家科技公司施压，要求其在其各种平台上对抗极端主义。
今年夏天，一系列谴责Facebook与美国和全球右翼暴力事件联系的报道层出不穷。
上月底，该公司曾收到警告，称一个民兵组织利用其平台在威斯康星州基诺沙市组织了一场反对反种族主义抗议的武装反示威活动，但未能删除该民兵组织的页面。
一名17岁的少年在基诺沙与一个民兵组织一起游行，后来开枪打死了两名抗议者，另一人受重伤。
今年夏天早些时候，TTP的一份报告详细描述了Facebook对其平台上“Boogaloo”账户激增的反应有多慢。TTP找到了100多页关于“Boogaloo”的想法，这个词在美国极右翼准军事团体中很流行，用来描述这个国家即将爆发的内战。
这些自我识别的“博格鲁人”在Facebook上讨论武器、战斗医学和如何开发炸药。
后来，TTP将这些Facebook群组的多名成员与现实世界的行为或暴力阴谋联系起来，其中包括三名被联邦调查局(FBI)逮捕的男子，他们涉嫌计划用燃烧弹袭击拉斯维加斯的黑人生命也是命(Black Lives Matter)抗议者。
TTP的负责人保罗说，她担心Facebook最近未能删除即将在波特兰举行的骄傲男孩活动的宣传页面，这可能意味着“更多的极端分子”出现在波特兰。
波特兰已经受到几个月来不断升级的政治紧张局势的打击，极右翼极端分子经常出现在那里骚扰和攻击正在进行的反种族主义示威者。
本月早些时候，特朗普支持者组成的大篷车开着卡车穿过这座城市，向街上的反示威者发射胡椒喷雾和彩弹枪。活动结束时，一位名叫迈克尔·雷诺尔(Michael Reinoehl)的自认为反法西斯的人开枪打死了一位名叫亚伦·“杰”·丹尼尔森(Aaron“Jay”Danielson)的特朗普支持者。
雷诺尔告诉副新闻，他开枪打死丹尼尔森是出于自卫，后来被华盛顿州的执法部门击毙。围绕他死亡的情况仍然不明朗，尽管一名目击者声称，雷诺尔在执法人员开枪打死他时没有对他们构成威胁。
即将在波特兰举行的骄傲男孩活动是在雷诺尔和丹尼尔森去世后宣布的，被宣传为“结束安提法”的集会。
注册会员成为创始会员，帮助塑造赫芬顿邮报的下一个篇章
note_en:
Members of the  Proud Boys and other far-right groups were using  Facebook this week to promote and raise funds for an upcoming rally in Portland, Oregon, despite recent assurances from the social media giant that such extremist organizations would not be able to organize on its platform, and after a summer marred by political violence linked to the company.
 On Wednesday multiple Facebook pages were active showing persons affiliated with the Proud Boys or other far-right groups promoting a   rally  against “antifa” and Black Lives Matter scheduled for Sept. 26 in Portland.
 Links to these Facebook pages were provided to HuffPost by the   Tech Transparency Project , a watchdog group. The links showed far-right groups posting propaganda videos promoting the upcoming rally, and included a page utilizing Facebook’s own fundraising tool to collect money for the event.
 HuffPost asked Facebook about the pages on Wednesday evening and by early Thursday afternoon all had been removed.
 “Proud Boys and promoting their events are banned from Facebook,” a Facebook spokesperson said. “We’ve already removed Events, Pages and accounts tied to the upcoming rally for this reason and our work to disrupt these efforts is ongoing.”
 But according to Katie Paul, director of the TTP, Facebook’s response is just yet another example of the company doing too little too late to combat extremism on its platform, removing harmful content only after being alerted to it by journalists.
 “Facebook has focused more effort on its public relations for cleaning up after a tragic event than it has to address how its platform is facilitating these events in the first place,” Paul told HuffPost.
 Among the pages removed by Facebook on Thursday was one called “Proud Security,” which was replete with Proud Boys symbols and imagery. The Proud Boys are a Trump-supporting,   neo-fascist street gang  infamous for attacking leftists at political rallies across the country,   often in Portland .
  On Sept. 8, the Proud Security page promoted the upcoming Proud Boys rally by posting a video montage of recent anti-racist protests in Portland, and imploring “patriots” from around the country to come to “take a stand” in the city.
 Two other pages removed Thursday belonged to far-right groups called the Patriot Coalition of Oregon and the Patriots of Washington. Both groups had posted a video montage of what is essentially Proud Boys riot porn, inviting supporters to come “smash antifa” in Portland at the Sept. 26 rally.
 The Proud Security page — run by a man named Daniel Tooze — utilized Facebook’s own fundraising tool to collect money for the event. Nearly $2,000 had been raised toward a $10,000 goal as of Wednesday evening.
 That page was also removed by Facebook on Thursday. Facebook’s  rules indicate that the company takes a fee from such fundraisers on its platform. The company did not respond to a  HuffPost question as to whether it had taken a fee from the Proud Boys fundraiser.
  On Wednesday, multiple celebrities took part in a highly publicized “Stop Hate For Profit” boycott of Instagram, which is owned by Facebook. It was the latest action in a monthslong campaign by a coalition of social justice groups to pressure the tech company into confronting extremism on its various platforms.
 This summer has seen a series of damning reports detailing Facebook’s ties to right-wing violence in the U.S. and across the globe.
 Late last month the company had been   warned  that a militia group was using its platform to organize an armed counter-demonstration against anti-racist protests in Kenosha, Wisconsin, but failed to remove the militia group’s page.
 A 17-year-old marching with a militia group in Kenosha later   shot and killed  two protesters, severely injuring another.
 Earlier this summer, a   report from TTP  detailed how slow Facebook had been in responding to the proliferation of “Boogaloo” accounts on its platform. TTP found over 100 pages devoted to the idea of the “Boogaloo,” a term popular among far-right paramilitary groups in the U.S. to describe an imminent civil war in this country.
 These self-identifying “Boogaloo bois”   used Facebook  to discuss weapons, combat medicine and how to develop explosives.
 Later TTP connected multiple members of these Facebook groups to real-world acts or plots of violence, including   three men arrested  by the FBI for allegedly planning to attack Black Lives Matter protesters in Las Vegas with Molotov cocktails.
 Paul, the director of TTP, said she’s worried Facebook’s recent failure to remove the pages promoting the upcoming Proud Boys event in Portland could mean “even more extremists” show up in the city.
 Portland is already reeling from months of escalating political tensions, which has seen far-right extremists   show up  to often harass and attack ongoing anti-racist demonstrators there.
 Earlier this month a caravan of Trump supporters   drove trucks through the city , firing pepper spray and paintball guns at counter-demonstrators in the streets. The event ended with a self-identifying anti-fascist named Michael Reinoehl shooting and killing a Trump supporter named Aaron “Jay” Danielson.
 Reinoehl, who told Vice News that he shot Danielson in self-defense, was later killed by law enforcement in Washington state. The circumstances surrounding his death   remain murky , although one witness claims Reinohl did not pose a threat to law enforcement officers when they shot him.
 The upcoming Proud Boys event in Portland was announced in the wake of Reinohl’s and Danielson’s deaths and is being billed as a rally to “end antifa.”

根据ProPublica和《纽约时报》杂志分析的荣鼎集团的最新数据，气温变暖和降雨变化将推动农业和温带气候北上，而海平面上升将消耗海岸线，危险的湿度水平将淹没密西西比河流域。
最近的其他研究表明，北美最宜居的气候将向北转移，全国范围内的大火发生率将会增加，这表明气候危机将深刻中断我们在美国的生活和耕作方式。看看人类几千年来居住的北美地区将会发生怎样的变化，以及你们国家将发生哪些变化。

by  Al Shaw,  Abrahm Lustgarten, ProPublica, and Jeremy W. Goldsmith, Special to ProPublica, September 15, 2020.
 ProPublica is a nonprofit newsroom that investigates abuses of power. Sign up to receive   our biggest stories as soon as they’re published.
 According to new data from the Rhodium Group analyzed by ProPublica and The New York Times Magazine, warming temperatures and changing rainfall will drive agriculture and temperate climates northward, while sea level rise will consume coastlines and dangerous levels of humidity will swamp the Mississippi River valley.
 Taken with other recent research showing that the most habitable climate in North America will shift northward and the incidence of large fires will increase across the country, this suggests that the climate crisis will  profoundly interrupt the way we live and farm in the United States. See how the North American places where humans have lived for thousands of years will shift and what changes are in store for your county.

以色列耶路撒冷-2020年7月1日-BiondVax制药有限公司。纳斯达克股票代码：BVXV)今天宣布，BiondVax的M-001通用流感候选疫苗第三阶段安慰剂对照、盲目、关键临床疗效试验的所有参与者现已完成现场参观。在过去的两个流感季节，总共有超过12,400名年龄在50岁以上的志愿者(其中一半年龄在65岁以上)在七个欧洲国家的83个地点参加了这项试验。这项研究的目的是评估M-001作为一种独立的非佐剂疫苗的能力，通过降低流感发病率(作为主要终点)和严重程度(作为次要终点)来提供针对流行流感病毒株的临床保护，并评估M-001的安全性。
季节性流感每年感染大约10-20%的世界人口，导致多达500万例严重疾病和65万人死亡。此外，大流行性流感，如2009年的H1N1猪流感大流行，是一个持续的全球威胁。然而，目前的流感疫苗针对的是流感病毒频繁变异的部分，因此必须每年更新，希望它们能与下一个流感季节的流行毒株相匹配，但在普通人群中平均只有40%的疫苗有效性，在老年人中的有效性低至12%。
BiondVax的M-001是高度保守的流感表位的单一重组蛋白。因此：
M-001不需要更新，因此可以全年制造和销售。
M-001旨在为现有和未来的季节性A和B毒株以及新出现的大流行毒株提供保护。
BiondVax首席科学家Tamar Ben-Yedida博士评论说：“我们很高兴，尽管新冠肺炎疫情仍在持续，而且在7个国家83个地点进行试验面临挑战，这要归功于所有相关人员--包括CRO、研究人员和数以千计的参与者--我们维持了我们关键的3期试验的计划时间表。鉴于正在进行的新冠肺炎大流行，可以说，对改进流感疫苗的需求从未如此明确过。为了更好地保护生命和经济，流感疫苗必须更有效地降低发病率和严重性。不用说，我们热切期待今年年底的审判结果。“。
试验第二个队列的参与者是在2019年/20年流感季节之前登记的，并在整个流感季节监测流感样疾病(ILI)症状。从感染ILI的参与者身上采集了拭子样本，目前正在由合格的实验室进行流感确认。分析将在未来几个月继续进行，预计2020年底会有结果。
作为这项第三阶段研究的一部分，M-001的细胞介导的免疫原性标记物将在一部分参与者中进行评估。美国国家过敏症和传染病研究所(NIAID)最近完成的临床研究报告(CSR)支持了M-001的第二阶段临床试验，结论是“M-001诱导了显著的多功能T细胞反应。”
除了正在进行的关键临床疗效试验外，BiondVax在耶路撒冷的试点设施的第三阶段试验、设备安装和制造工艺的扩大也在进行中。该设施计划的年散装能力高达1000万至2000万剂。
BiondVax公司(纳斯达克市场代码：BVXV)是一家第三阶段临床阶段生物制药公司，正在开发一种通用流感疫苗。这种名为M-001的候选疫苗旨在针对当前和未来的季节性和大流行性流感提供多毒株和多季节保护。BiondVax的专利技术利用保守的和普通的流感病毒肽的独特组合，旨在刺激免疫系统的两个手臂，以实现交叉保护和持久的效果。在总共7个完成的1/2期和2期临床试验中，共招募了818名参与者，该疫苗已被证明是安全的、耐受性良好的和免疫原性的。正在进行的关键的3期临床试验旨在评估M-001在减少流感疾病和严重程度方面的安全性和有效性。欲了解更多信息，请访问www.biondvax.com。

Jerusalem, Israel – July 1, 2020 –  BiondVax Pharmaceuticals Ltd. (Nasdaq: BVXV) today announced that all participants in the placebo-controlled, blinded, pivotal, clinical efficacy, Phase 3 trial of BiondVax’s M‑001 universal influenza vaccine candidate have now completed their site visits. In total, over 12,400 volunteers aged 50+ (with half aged 65+) were enrolled in the trial over the past two flu seasons in 83 sites across seven European countries. The purpose of the study is to assess M-001’s ability as a standalone non-adjuvanted vaccine to provide clinical protection from circulating influenza strains as measured by reduction of influenza illness rate (as a primary endpoint) and severity (as a secondary endpoint), as well as to assess M-001’s safety.
 Seasonal influenza annually infects approximately  10-20% of the world’s population resulting in up to about  five million cases of severe illness and 650,000 deaths. In addition, pandemic influenza, such as the H1N1 Swine Flu pandemic of 2009, is a constant global threat. However, current influenza vaccines, which target frequently mutating parts of the flu virus and therefore must be updated annually in the hope they will match the next flu season’s circulating strains, achieve on average only about  40% vaccine effectiveness in the general population and as low as  12% in older adults.
 BiondVax’s M-001 is a single recombinant protein of highly conserved influenza epitopes. Consequently:
 M-001 does not need to be updated and therefore can be manufactured and distributed year-round.
 M-001 is designed to provide protection to both existing and future seasonal A and B strains, as well as emerging pandemic strains.
 Dr. Tamar Ben-Yedida, BiondVax’s Chief Scientist, commented, “ We are pleased that despite the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, and the challenge of conducting the trial across 83 sites and seven countries, thanks to all the people involved – including the CRO, investigators, and thousands of participants – we have maintained the planned timelines of our pivotal Phase 3 trial. In light of the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, the need for improved influenza vaccines has arguably never been clearer. To better protect lives and economies, influenza vaccines must be more effective in reducing illness rates and severity. Needless to say, we are eagerly anticipating results of our trial by the end of this year.”
 Participants in the trial’s second cohort were enrolled prior to the 2019/20 flu season and monitored for influenza-like illness (ILI) symptoms throughout the flu season. Swabs samples were collected from those participants with ILI, and influenza confirmation is currently being conducted by a qualified laboratory. Analysis will continue in the coming months, and results are expected by the end of 2020.
 As part of this Phase 3 study, cell-mediated immunogenicity markers of M-001 will be evaluated in a subset of participants. The recently completed clinical study report (CSR) of a U.S. National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID) supported Phase 2 clinical trial of M-001 concluded that, “  M-001 induced significant polyfunctional T cell responses.”
 In addition to the ongoing pivotal, clinical efficacy, Phase 3 trial, equipment installation and manufacturing process scale-up in BiondVax’s pilot facility in Jerusalem are in progress. The facility has planned annual capacity of up to between 10 and 20 million doses in bulk.
 About BiondVax  BiondVax (NASDAQ: BVXV) is a Phase 3 clinical stage biopharmaceutical company developing a universal flu vaccine. The vaccine candidate, called M-001, is designed to provide multi-strain and multi-season protection against current and future, seasonal and pandemic influenza. BiondVax’s proprietary technology utilizes a unique combination of conserved and common influenza virus peptides intended to stimulate both arms of the immune system for a cross-protecting and long-lasting effect. In a total of seven completed Phase 1/2 and Phase 2 clinical trials enrolling 818 participants, the vaccine has been shown to be safe, well-tolerated, and immunogenic. The ongoing pivotal Phase 3 clinical trial aims to assess safety and effectiveness of M-001 in reducing flu illness and severity. For more information, please visit  www.biondvax.com.
  Forward Looking Statements  This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of the Private Litigation Reform Act of 1995. Words such as “expect,” “believe,” “intend,” “plan,” “continue,” “may,” “will,” “anticipate,” and similar expressions are intended to identify forward-looking statements. Risks and uncertainties include, but are not limited to, risks relating to the COVID-19 (coronavirus) pandemic, including a risk of delay in the availability of the top line results from our pivotal clinical efficacy Phase 3 trial for M-001, the prosecution, timing and results of the ongoing Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials and any subsequent trials; timing of receipt of regulatory approval of our manufacturing facility in Jerusalem; ability to demonstrate the efficacy and safety of the vaccine; the timing of clinical trials and marketing approvals; the risk that drug development involves a lengthy and expensive process with uncertain outcome; the ability of the Company to maintain, preserve and defend its intellectual property and patents granted; whether our  vaccine candidate will successfully advance through the clinical trial process on a timely basis, or at all, and receive approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration or equivalent foreign regulatory agencies; the adequacy of available cash resources and the ability to raise additional capital when needed. 跳跃导航跳跃搜索直接聚变驱动器(DFD)是一种概念性的低放射性核聚变火箭发动机，旨在为行星际航天器提供推力和电力。这一概念基于塞缪尔·A·科恩(Samuel A.Cohen)于2002年发明的普林斯顿场反转配置反应堆，目前正在美国能源部设施普林斯顿等离子体物理实验室进行建模和实验测试，并由普林斯顿卫星系统公司进行建模和评估。截至2018年，该概念已进入第二阶段[需要澄清]，以进一步推进设计。
直接聚变驱动器(DFD)是一种概念性的聚变动力航天器发动机，因其无需经过中间发电步骤就能从聚变中产生推力而得名。DFD使用一种新型的磁约束和加热系统，以氦-3(He-3)和氘(D)的混合物为燃料，产生高比功率、可变推力和比冲，以及一个低辐射航天器推进系统。[1]当原子核在高温(100keV或1120000K)等离子体中由一种物质组成，包括电子和离子的带电粒子的集合，结合(或融合)在一起，释放出巨大的能量时，聚变就会发生。在DFD系统中，等离子体被限制在线性螺线管线圈内部的环状磁场中，并被旋转磁场加热到熔化温度。[1]从等离子体发射的韧致辐射和同步辐射被捕获并转换成电能，用于通信、航天器站位和维持等离子体的温度。[2]此设计使用特殊形状的无线电波(RF)天线来加热等离子体。该设计还包括一个可充电电池或一个氘氧辅助电源单元，用于启动或重新启动DFD。[1]。
捕获的辐射能量加热到1,500°K(1,230°C；2,240°F)，这是一种以含硼结构流出等离子体的He-Xe流体。这些能量通过闭环布雷顿循环发电机转化为电能，用于为线圈供电、为射频加热器供电、为电池充电、通信和站位保持功能。[1]将推进剂添加到边缘等离子体流中，在引导和加速通过磁喷嘴时会产生可变的推力和比冲；通过喷嘴的这种动量流主要由离子在通过磁喷嘴和向外扩张时携带，因此起到离子推进器的作用。[1]。
该实验研究装置的建造和大部分早期运行都是由美国能源部资助的。最近的研究-第一阶段和第二阶段-由NASA高级概念研究所(NIAC)计划资助。2001年至2008年间发表了一系列关于这一概念的文章；2007年报告了第一批实验结果。从2012年开始，发表了许多关于航天器飞行任务(第一阶段)的研究报告。2017年，该团队报告说，使用这种方法对电子加热的研究已经超过了理论预测，第二代机器中测量离子加热的实验正在进行中。[1]截至2018年，该概念已经进入第二阶段，以进一步推进设计。[4][5]全尺寸单元的直径约为2米，长约10米。[6]。
斯蒂芬妮·托马斯(Stephanie Thomas)是普林斯顿卫星系统公司的副总裁，也是直接聚变驱动器(Direct Fusion Drive)的首席研究员。[7]。
分析预测，直接聚变驱动器每产生每兆瓦的聚变功率将产生5-10牛顿[1]推力[5]，比冲(Isp)约为10,000秒，可用作电力的功率为200千瓦。[4]大约35%的聚变功率用于推力，30%用于电力，25%用于加热，10%用于射频加热。[1]。
建模显示，这项技术可能会在4年内将质量约为1000千克(2200千克)的航天器推向冥王星。[4]由于DFD在一个集成设备中提供动力和推进，它还将在到达时为有效载荷提供高达2 mW的功率，扩大了仪器选择、激光/光通信、[1][4]的选择范围，甚至可以通过波长为1080 nm的激光束将高达50千瓦的功率从轨道器传输到着陆器。[1]。
设计者认为这项技术可以从根本上扩展行星任务的科学能力。[4]有人建议将这种双动力/推进技术用于冥王星轨道器和着陆器飞行任务，[1][4]以及在猎户座航天器上进行集成，以便在相对较短的时间内将载人飞行任务运送到火星[8][9](4个月，而不是目前技术下的9个月)。[6]

note_en:
Jump to navigation  Jump to search  Direct Fusion Drive ( DFD) is a conceptual low radioactivity, nuclear-fusion  rocket engine designed to produce both  thrust and electric power for interplanetary spacecraft. The concept is based on the  Princeton field-reversed configuration reactor invented in 2002 by Samuel A. Cohen, and is being modeled and experimentally tested at  Princeton Plasma Physics Laboratory, a US Department of Energy facility, and modeled and evaluated by Princeton Satellite Systems. As of 2018, the concept has moved on to Phase II [   clarification needed] to further advance the design.
   The Direct Fusion Drive (DFD) is a conceptual fusion-powered spacecraft engine, named for its ability to produce thrust from fusion without going through an intermediary electricity-generating step. The DFD uses a novel magnetic confinement and heating system, fueled with a mixture of  helium-3 (He-3) and  deuterium (D), to produce a high specific power, variable thrust and specific impulse, and a low-radiation spacecraft propulsion system.  [1] Fusion happens when  atomic nuclei, comprising one species in a hot (100 keV or 1,120,000,00 K)  plasma, a collection of electrically charged particles that includes electrons and ions, join  (or fuse) together, releasing enormous amounts of energy. In the DFD system, the plasma is confined in a torus-like magnetic field inside of a linear solenoidal coil and is heated by a rotating magnetic field to fusion temperatures.  [1] Bremsstrahlung and synchrotron radiation emitted from the plasma are captured and converted to electricity for communications, spacecraft station-keeping, and maintaining the plasma's temperature.  [2] This design uses a specially shaped  radio waves (RF) "antenna" to heat the plasma.  The design also includes a rechargeable battery or a  deuterium-oxygen auxiliary power unit to startup or restart DFD.  [1]
 The captured radiated energy heats to 1,500 K (1,230 °C; 2,240 °F) a He-Xe fluid that flows outside the plasma in a boron-containing structure. That energy is put through a closed-loop  Brayton cycle generator to transform it into electricity for use in energizing the coils,powering the RF heater, charging the battery, communications, and station-keeping functions.  [1] Adding propellant to the edge plasma flow results in a variable  thrust and specific impulse when channeled and accelerated through a  magnetic nozzle; this flow of momentum past the nozzle is predominantly carried by the  ions as they expand through the magnetic nozzle and beyond, and thus, function as an  ion thruster.  [1]
  The construction of the experimental research device and most of its early operations were funded by the US Department of Energy. The recent studies —Phase I and Phase II— are funded by the  NASA Institute for Advanced Concepts (NIAC) program.  A series of articles on the concept were published between 2001 and 2008; the first experimental results were reported in 2007. Numerous studies of spacecraft missions (Phase I) were published, beginning in 2012. In 2017 the team reported that "Studies of electron heating with this method have surpassed theoretical predictions, and experiments to measure ion heating in the second-generation machine are ongoing."  [1] As of 2018, the concept has moved on to Phase II to further advance the design.  [4]  [5] The full-size unit would measure approximately 2 m in diameter and 10 m long.  [6]
 Stephanie Thomas is vice president of Princeton Satellite Systems and also the Principal Investigator for the Direct Fusion Drive.  [7]
  Analyses predict that the Direct Fusion Drive would produce between 5-10  Newtons  [1] thrust per each  MW of generated fusion power,  [5] with a  specific impulse (I sp) of about 10,000 seconds and 200 kW available as electrical power.  [4] Approximately 35% of the fusion power goes to thrust, 30% to electric power, 25% lost to heat, and 10% is recirculated for the RF heating.  [1]
 Modeling shows that this technology can potentially propel a spacecraft with a mass of about 1,000 kg (2,200 lb) to  Pluto in 4 years.  [4] Since DFD provides power as well as propulsion in one integrated device, it would also provide as much as 2 MW of power to the payloads upon arrival, expanding options for instrument selection,  laser/optical communications,  [1]  [4] and even transfer up to 50 kW of power from the orbiter to the lander through a  laser beam operating at 1080 nm wavelength.  [1]
 The designers think that this technology can radically expand the science capability of planetary missions.  [4] This dual power/propulsion technology has been suggested to be used on a  Pluto orbiter and lander mission,  [1]  [4] a well as integration on the  Orion spacecraft to transport a  crewed mission to Mars in a relatively short time  [8]  [9] (4 months instead of 9 with current technology). 周三，PlayStation5的新闻铺天盖地，主要围绕着11月12日与游戏机一起发布的全新内容。这场争斗中遗失了一个关键细节，证实了8月份泄露的PS5：它缺乏全面的向后兼容支持。
在周三的一次采访中，日本游戏杂志Famitsu向索尼互动娱乐公司(Sony Interactive Entertainment)首席执行官吉姆·瑞安(Jim Ryan)询问了这款新游戏机与PS1、PS2和PS3光盘的兼容性或下载情况。瑞安解释说，PS5专用工程意味着设计团队主要专注于同时使用高速固态硬盘和新的DualSense控制器。
瑞安告诉Famitsu，这阻碍了索尼提供与老款游戏机的兼容性，尽管他明确表示，索尼希望通过开发与PS4游戏的兼容性来支持PlayStation4和1亿玩家，因为我们认为他们也会喜欢在PS5上玩PS4游戏。
这一声明并没有澄清购买PS4使用的PS1游戏是否会转移到PS5。它也没有提到玩家现有的从PlayStation now云订阅服务向PS4流式传输老一代游戏的能力，也没有提到我们是否应该期待这一功能在11月无缝转移到PS5上。SIE的代表没有立即回复我们关于这些可能的功能的问题。
瑞安目前关于PS4向后兼容性的说法令人好奇，因为他之前曾嘲笑过老一辈游戏机的喜爱。正如他在2017年接受《时代》杂志采访时所说：
当我们已经涉足向后兼容性的时候，我可以说这是那些要求很多，但实际上用得不多的功能之一。我最近参加了Gran Turismo的一个活动，那里有PS1，PS2，PS3和PS4游戏，PS1和PS2游戏，它们看起来很古老，像是为什么会有人玩这个？
瑞安对老一辈人的看法可能并没有得益于2018年PlayStation Classic平淡无奇的销售(尽管可以说，这些销售更多的是关于不可靠的执行，而不是经典游戏)。
PlayStation4是该公司第一款没有内置向后兼容光盘游戏支持的游戏机，而且与PlayStation3上首次推出的纯下载软件不兼容。这意味着你必须保留旧游戏机才能访问那些无光盘游戏。在此之前，PS3在其生命周期的早期接受了一次重大修订，取消了对PS2光盘的支持(因为这一功能运行在PS3内置到早期PS3中的情感引擎架构上，该架构被转储以削减成本)。
相比之下，微软在Xbox One和Xbox系列游戏机上都通过向后兼容的促销活动积极争取老游戏的粉丝。这意味着Xbox生态系统中的游戏玩家可以在多代游戏机上原生访问之前的购买(无论是光盘形式还是数字购买)，并在逐个游戏的基础上进行分辨率和性能升级。我们仍在等待性能问题困扰的PS4游戏是否会接受PS5特定升级，尽管PS4 Pro的可选增强模式已经明确表示，某些游戏(咳嗽、咳嗽、血腥)需要特殊补丁才能利用更新的硬件。
周三发布的PlayStation5新闻并没有进一步详细说明PS4游戏在即将推出的游戏机上进行的额外升级。相反，我们了解到一些主要的PlayStation5游戏，特别是地平线：被禁止的西部和漫威的蜘蛛侠：迈尔斯·莫拉莱斯，将在PS5和PS4上同时推出。这似乎与索尼最近的言论背道而驰，即维持世代游戏，而不是为其最大的游戏支持Xbox风格的向前兼容计划。
note_en:
Wednesday's deluge of PlayStation 5 news mostly revolved around brand-new content launching alongside the console on November 12. Lost in the fray was a key detail that confirmed  an August leak about PS5: its lack of sweeping backward-compatibility support.
 In  a Wednesday interview, Japanese gaming magazine Famitsu asked Sony Interactive Entertainment chief Jim Ryan about the new console's compatibility with PS1, PS2, and PS3 discs or downloads. Ryan explained that "PS5-specific engineering" meant the design team was mostly focused on "the simultaneous use of high-speed SSDs and the new DualSense controller."
 This prevented Sony from delivering compatibility with older consoles, Ryan told Famitsu, even though he made clear that Sony wanted to support PlayStation 4's "100 million players" by developing compatibility with "99%" of PS4 games, since "we thought that they would like to play PS4 titles on the PS5, as well."
  This announcement doesn't clarify whether PS1 games purchased for use on PS4 will transfer to PS5. It also doesn't mention the existing ability for players to stream older-generation games to PS4 from the PlayStation Now cloud-subscription service or whether we should expect that functionality to seamlessly transfer to PS5 in November. SIE reps did not immediately respond to our questions about those possible features.
 Ryan's current statement about affection for PS4 backward compatibility is curious, considering he has previously scoffed at affection for older console generations. As he told  Time Magazine in 2017:
 When we've dabbled with backward compatibility, I can say it is one of those features that is much requested, but not actually used much. That, and I was at a   Gran Turismo event recently where they had PS1, PS2, PS3 and PS4 games, and the PS1 and the PS2 games, they looked ancient, like why would anybody play this?
 Ryan's take about that older generation probably wasn't helped by lackluster PlayStation Classic sales in 2018 (though those were arguably less about classic games and more about  iffy execution).
  PlayStation 4 was the company's first console to not ship with native backward-compatibility support for disc-based games, along with incompatibility with download-only software that debuted on PlayStation 3. That meant you had to hold on to your old console to access those disc-less games. This followed the PS3 receiving a major revision early in its lifetime to remove support for PS2 discs (due to this feature running on  PS2 "Emotion Engine" architecture built into early PS3s, which was dumped to cut costs).
 Comparatively, Microsoft has aggressively courted fans of older games with a backward-compatibility sales pitch on both Xbox One and  Xbox Series consoles. That means gamers in the Xbox ecosystem can expect native access to previous purchases (whether in disc form or as a digital purchase) across multiple console generations  and resolution and performance upgrades on a game-by-game basis. We're still waiting to hear whether PS4 games that struggled with performance issues might receive PS5-specific upgrades, though the  PS4 Pro's optional "boost mode" already made clear that certain games (cough, cough,   Bloodborne) would need special patches to take advantage of newer hardware.
 Wednesday's dump of PlayStation 5 news did not go into further detail about additional boosts to PS4 games as played on the upcoming console. Instead, we learned that some major PlayStation 5 games, particularly  Horizon: Forbidden West and  Marvel's Spider-Man: Miles Morales, will launch simultaneously on PS5 and PS4. This appears to run somewhat counter to Sony's recent comments about  maintaining "generations" instead of supporting an Xbox-style "forward-compatible" plan for its biggest games.

“有时候我回想起来，我想，我们到底在想什么？但说真的，我希望--就像其他企业家听到的那样--你确实会想，‘嘿，我怎么才能更大胆呢？’因为实际上，这才是伟大的源泉。“扎特林说。
也许也就不足为奇了，她发现，当团队在2009年夏天前往旧金山湾区，试图销售一种产品，试图将一项以前只有当时的互联网巨头才能提供的服务民主化，但并不是每个人都准备好了。
“很多人都目瞪口呆，”她说。他们对我们不屑一顾，因为我们真的很有远见--我们当然不缺乏远见。所以这是很多对话，但也有一些对话，人们真的倾身而入，他们会说：‘哦，哇，告诉我更多。’也许他们对网络安全了解很多，或者他们真的很担心互联网的民主化和躲在戒备森严的围墙后面。那些人真的很兴奋。我们就是这样找到最初的投资者或最初的队友的。“。
随着时间的推移，团队找到了投资者和最初的一批员工，但这并不容易。“当时很大胆，并不是每个人都认为这是个好主意。有些人看我们的眼神好像我们疯了，但我想说的是，当人们看你像疯了一样时，你很可能是在做大事。“。
虽然早期很多人将Cloudflare视为CDN，但它始终是一个需要CDN才能工作的安全产品(因此，该公司有时将自己描述为“意外的CDN”)。十年前，焦点主要集中在网络垃圾邮件发送者身上，因为这是每个人都可以涉足的事情，它在宣传资料中非常明确地表明了自己打击垃圾邮件的能力。
“我们的宣讲台上有一张幻灯片，上面引用了来自一些中型企业、小企业和开发人员的现实生活中的IT管理员的话，用他们自己的话--以及他们非常严厉的话--解释了他们有多么鄙视这些网上的网络威胁。”[…]。即使你们对网络安全或全球性能一无所知，你们也都能理解，哇，这里面有东西，对吧？“。当然，当时没有针对这些威胁的真正保护措施，这是有帮助的。
Zatlyn指出，尽管如此，获得早期客户也并不容易，因为一些人只是不了解公司在做什么。但该团队将其作为反馈，并改进了其解释方式。
“在创业初期，你需要尝试很多事情。你不需要知道你业务的每一个角落，但你需要AVE才能真正拥有很高的学习率。实际上，那是企业家最好的朋友。你能以多快的速度学习，你能以多快的速度接受反馈并对其进行迭代？“。
你可以看下面的对话，包括扎特林对上市的想法：
note_en:
“Sometimes I think back and I think, what were we thinking? But really, I hope — as other entrepreneurs listen — that you do think, ‘hey, how can I be more bold?’ Because actually, that’s where a lot of greatness comes from,” Zatlyn said.
 It’s maybe no surprise then that she found that when the team went to the Bay Area in the summer of 2009, trying to sell a product that democratized a service that was previously only available to the internet giants of that time, not everybody was ready.
  “A lot of eyes glazed over,” she said. They kind of brushed us aside because we really had a big vision — we certainly did not lack in vision. So that was a lot of the conversations, but there were some conversations where people really leaned in and they’re like: ‘Oh, wow, tell me more.’ And maybe they knew a lot about cybersecurity or maybe they were really worried about the democratization of the internet and going behind guarded walls. And those people really got excited. And that’s how we found kind of our initial investors or initial teammates.”
  Over time, the team found investors and an initial set of employees, but it wasn’t easy. “It was bold then and not everyone thought it was a good idea. Some people looked at us like we’re crazy, but I’d like to say that when people look at you like you’re crazy, you’re probably onto something big.”
 While a lot of people looked at Cloudflare as a CDN in its early days, it was always meant to be a security product that needed the CDN to work (the company sometimes describes itself as an ‘accidental CDN’ because of that). Ten years ago, the focus was mostly on web spammers and because that was something everybody could relate to, it made its ability to combat that very explicit in its pitch deck.
 “We literally had a slide in our pitch deck that had quotes from real-life IT administrators from some medium-sized businesses, small businesses and developers, explaining in their own words — and their very hard-hitting words — how much they despise like these cyber threats that were online. […] Even if you knew nothing about cybersecurity or global performance, you could all understand wow, there’s something here, right?” And of course, it helped that there was no real protection against these threats available at the time.
 Still, getting early customers didn’t come easy, either, Zatlyn noted, because some just didn’t understand what the company was doing. But the team took that as feedback and improved how it explained itself.
 “Early on as an entrepreneur, you got to try a lot of things. And you don’t need to know every single corner of your business, but you need ave to really have a high rate of learning. Actually, that’s an entrepreneur’s best friend. How fast can you learn, how fast can you take the feedback and iterate on it?”
 You can watch the rest of the conversation, including Zatlyn’s thoughts about going public, below:

这个故事是2020年选举的一部分，CNET报道了11月投票前的准备情况。这位联邦调查局局长周四在国会听证会上表示，如果Facebook页面或Instagram帖子提供了你的投票站位置，你应该与当地的选举办公室再次核实。更好的是，根本不要从社交媒体上获取选举信息。
周四，众议院国土安全委员会(House Committee On Homeland Security)主持了一年一度的全球威胁听证会，情报机构在过去几年的听证会上对国际网络攻击和网络虚假信息发出了警告。
为我们的编辑订阅CNET Now时事通讯，精选当天最重要的新闻。
造谣运动仍然是周四听证会的焦点。联邦调查局局长克里斯托弗·雷警告说，俄罗斯正寻求通过在社交媒体上攻击民主党候选人乔·拜登来影响2020年美国总统大选。
雷的证词证实了国家情报总监办公室的调查结果，该办公室在8月份表示，俄罗斯人正试图破坏这位前副总统的候选人资格。
这位联邦调查局局长表示，Facebook和Twitter等社交网络已经与FBI合作，以取缔虚假信息活动，但他也警告美国人不要在这些平台上获得选举信息。
雷说，在选举背景下，当美国人自己在社交媒体上获取选举信息的错误时，这是我们特别关注的问题。我们正在努力确保美国人知道，要想获得有关你在哪里、何时以及如何投票的信息，你需要访问当地选举官员的网站。不要从社交媒体上窃取信息。
Facebook在一份声明中表示，其选举信息中心的细节直接来自雷提到的消息来源。一位公司发言人表示，我们投票信息中心的一个重要组成部分是我们链接到州选举官员和其他无党派公民组织，并从他们那里获取信息。到11月，我们将继续与州选举官员密切合作，确保各中心及时了解各州的最新选举信息。
造谣运动并不局限于攻击政客或政策。当为克里姆林宫支持的巨魔农场工作的俄罗斯人在2016年大选前在Facebook和Twitter上冒充美国人时，帖子中包含了显示选民投票日期错误的模因。在Facebook上，这样的帖子达到了大约1.26亿美国人。
这种策略仍然盛行，TikTok上的一个保守派账户告诉民主党人在选举日的第二天投票。
尽管有雷的警告，近五分之一的美国成年人还是从社交媒体上获取政治新闻。
Facebook和Twitter都创建了自己的选举信息中心，以提供准确的投票信息。如果您正在寻找当地官员的选举信息，您可以使用此目录来查找。
这位联邦调查局局长告诉国会，他对选举安全最大的担忧是，美国人可能会因为网上的错误信息而对民主失去信心。
雷说：我担心人们会因为噪音和混乱而产生一种徒劳无益的感觉。这是一个很难解决的问题。
尊重他人，保持文明礼貌，不离题。我们删除违反我们政策的评论，我们鼓励您阅读这些评论。我们可以随时关闭讨论线程。
note_en:
This story is part of  Elections 2020, CNET's coverage of the run-up to voting in November.    If a Facebook page or an Instagram post is offering the location of your polling place, you should double check that with your local elections office, the FBI director said Thursday at a congressional hearing. Better yet, don't get your election information from social media at all.
 The  House Committee on Homeland Security hosted on Thursday its annual worldwide threats hearing, where intelligence agencies in past years have warned about  international cyberattacks and  online disinformation.
  Subscribe to the CNET Now newsletter for our editors' picks of the most important stories of the day.
 Disinformation campaigns remained a focus of Thursday's hearing. FBI Director Christopher Wray warned that Russia is looking to influence the 2020 US presidential election by  attacking Democratic nominee Joe Biden on social media.
                                             Wray's testimony confirms findings from the  Office of the Director of National Intelligence, which said in August that Russians are trying to undermine the former vice president's candidacy.
 The FBI director said that social networks like Facebook and Twitter have worked with the bureau to take down disinformation campaigns, but he also warned Americans against getting election information on those platforms.
 "It's particularly of concern to us in the election context when Americans make the mistake of getting information about elections themselves on social media," Wray said. "We're trying to make sure Americans know [that] to get information about where, when and how you vote, you need to go to your local election official's website. Don't take it from social media."
  Facebook said in a statement that details for   its own election information hub come directly from the sources that Wray mentions.   "An important part of our Voting Information Center is that we link to and source information from state election officials and other nonpartisan civic organizations," a company spokesperson said. "And we'll continue to work closely with state election officials through November to ensure the centers are updated with the latest election information in each state."
  Disinformation campaigns aren't limited to attacks against politicians or policies. When  Russians working for a Kremlin-backed troll farm posed as Americans on Facebook and Twitter ahead of the 2016 election, posts included memes showing the wrong polling date for voters. On Facebook, such posts reached  about 126 million Americans.
 That tactic is still prevalent, with a conservative account on TikTok telling Democrats to  vote the day after Election Day.
 Despite Wray's warning, nearly one in five American adults get their  political news from social media.
 Facebook and  Twitter have both created their own election information hubs to deliver accurate information about voting. If you're looking for election information from your local officials, you can use  this directory to find it.
 The FBI director told Congress his biggest concern related to election security is that Americans could lose confidence in democracy because of misinformation online.
 "I worry people will take on a feeling of futility because of all the noise and confusion that's generated," Wray said. "And that's a very hard problem to combat."

title: 联邦调查局局长：在社交媒体上获取选举信息是错误的
title_en: FBI Director: It's a Mistake To Get Election Information on Social Media

 (long message...sorry)I apologize for the surprise and suddenness of this code. I have been working as a developer for Network Associates on a PGP plug-in for Mozilla for some time, and was not aware of the work detailed in this bug until today. I was directed by Jean-Francois Ducarroz < ducarroz@netscape.com> to attach the diffs and files to this bug, which I have done. Jean-Francois is apparently the libmime module owner these days.The code I wrote basically adds a content type handler and registers itself to handle multipart/encrypted, multipart/signed, and plain/text. The idea is that the code scans these messages for PGP content, and if it finds any, it attempts to process it. If not, it creates a new mime object and passes the message along, de-registering itself from the above types temporarily so that the default handlers will take over.I also added an overlay for the Composer window, with a PGP menu in it. This is a hard-coded reference in the messengercompose.xul file. 本周早些时候，我们报道了TikTok/甲骨文交易中涉及的绝对欺诈行为。与甲骨文收购TikTok是为了满足总统违宪的要求，即中国公司ByteDance将TikTok出售给一家美国公司的说法相反，这篇报道显示，这只是甲骨文云服务的托管交易，甲骨文的云服务在顶级云提供商的名单上遥遥领先。
最终的结果是没有真正的出售(尽管财政部仍在审查这笔交易)，而是甲骨文的一份大合同，以及一个虚假的故事，总统可以假装他强迫ByteDance出售TikTok，尽管它保留了该公司的所有权(有传言称，托管交易将包括甲骨文的一小部分股权，很可能是象征性的股权)。
我在文章中提到的另一个关键点是，自从特朗普就任总统以来，甲骨文的高管领导层，从拉里·埃里森(Larry Ellison)开始，但包括首席执行官萨夫拉·卡茨(Safra Catz)，一直在讨好特朗普和白宫。虽然硅谷的许多高管团队已经相当清楚地表示，他们对特朗普担任总统有多不舒服，但甲骨文…。结果却适得其反。虽然我把事情发展成这样很方便，但“华尔街日报”(Wall Street Journal)的一篇报道强调了这是如何从第一天起就是一场骗局。
一位知情人士说，甲骨文最初被带入谈判，是为了提供微软公司(Microsoft Corp.)的替代方案，微软公司是与沃尔玛(Walmart)合作的竞争对手。这位知情人士说，ByteDance的现有投资者、美国投资公司红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)和通用大西洋公司(General Atlantic)一直在寻找一家与政府关系密切的科技公司，并最终选择了甲骨文。
正如我们早些时候讨论的那样，从这场愚蠢的磨难的第一天开始，总统就明确表示，他将把一笔交易交给一家他个人喜欢的公司(显然是指领导层舔他的靴子的公司)。毕竟，在最初表示要在美国屏蔽TikTok之后，随着微软参与的传言传出，特朗普立即表示将阻止与微软的交易。微软不得不在一个周末向特朗普卑躬屈膝，才让他勉强表示，他们可以继续寻求交易。
当然，还有沃尔玛的故事。有传言称沃尔玛是领先的竞标者，但特朗普的白宫介入并告诉他们，他们也会阻止这笔交易，因为这将表明特朗普关于禁止TikTok的国家安全解释是一个明显的谎言。
换句话说，在整个无稽之谈的过程中，总统--他还一度要求任何交易都要收取发现者的费用--明确表示这是他自己的突发奇想。他强迫出售，他要挑选一个赢家--赢家必须是他喜欢的人。
这似乎不是人们欣赏的那种自由市场。这似乎是腐败的裙带资本主义制度中最糟糕的。
“华尔街日报”的这篇文章详细描述了整个过程是如何腐败的。这一切都是为了迎合特朗普的自负，并在他的竞选活动的银行账户中划线：
知情人士说，红杉资本(Sequoia Capital)利用自己的人脉推动政府允许TikTok继续在美国运营。该公司的全球执行合伙人道格·里昂(Doug Leone)代表TikTok牵头游说，他和妻子在本选举季向共和党候选人捐赠了数万美元，包括总统的连任竞选活动。今年1月，里昂还在他硅谷的家中为国务卿迈克·庞皮欧(Mike Pompeo)举行了招待会。
据与白宫有联系的人士称，卡茨与特朗普的关系可能会让甲骨文在交易获得批准方面占据优势。甲骨文与美国政府合作了几十年，包括与国家安全机构签订了多份合同。
再一次，这只是总统的表演性废话，以他自己的支持者中饱私囊而告终。所有这些似乎都与实际的国家安全没有任何关系。这是个笑话。
感谢您阅读这篇技术污垢帖子。这几天有这么多事情争相吸引大家的注意力，我们真的很感谢您抽出时间来参加我们的活动。我们每天都在努力工作，为我们的社区提供高质量的内容。
Techdle是为数不多的真正独立的媒体机构之一。我们背后没有一家大公司，我们在很大程度上依赖我们的社区来支持我们，在这个时代，广告商对赞助小型独立网站越来越不感兴趣-特别是像我们这样不愿在报告和分析方面出拳的网站。
虽然其他网站已经求助于付费墙、注册要求和越来越烦人的/侵入性广告，但我们一直保持Techdle对任何人开放和可用。但是为了继续这样做，我们需要你们的支持。我们为我们的读者提供了多种方式来支持我们，从直接捐款到特别订阅和酷商品-每一点都有帮助。谢谢。
note_en:
Earlier this week we wrote about the  absolute grift involved in the TikTok / Oracle deal. Contrary to the framing that this was Oracle "buying" TikTok to satisfy the President's unconstitutional demand that the Chinese company ByteDance sell TikTok to an American company, the story showed that this was just a hosting deal for Oracle's cloud service, which is  way down the list of top cloud providers.
 The end result was no actual sale (though the Treasury Department is still "reviewing" the deal), but a big contract for Oracle, and a bogus story in which the President can pretend he forced ByteDance to "sell" TikTok, even though it retains ownership in the company (there are some rumors that the hosting deal will include a small, and probably symbolic, equity stake for Oracle).
 The other key point I noted in my article was that Oracle's executive leadership, starting with Larry Ellison, but including CEO Safra Catz, have been cozying up to Trump and the White House ever since Trump became President. While much of Silicon Valley's executive teams have made it quite clear how uncomfortable they are with a Trump Presidency, Oracle... has done the opposite. And while I framed it as being convenient that things worked out this way, a report from the Wall Street Journal  highlights how this was the grift from day one.
 Oracle was originally brought into the negotiations to provide an alternative to Microsoft Corp., a rival bidder with Walmart as a partner, said one person familiar with the talks. The U.S. investment firms Sequoia Capital and General Atlantic, which are existing investors in ByteDance,  went in search of a tech company with close ties to the administration and settled on Oracle, the person said.
 As we discussed earlier, from day one of this silly ordeal, the President made it clear he would steer a deal to a company he personally liked (meaning one where the leadership licked his boots, apparently). After all, after first saying that he was going to block TikTok in the US, as rumors of Microsoft's involvement came out, Trump immediately said that  he would block a deal with Microsoft. Microsoft had to go  grovelling to Trump over a weekend to have him say, grudgingly, they could continue to pursue a deal.
 Then, of course, there's the Walmart story. Rumors came out that Walmart was a leading bidder, but Trump's White House stepped in and  told them they would block that deal too, because it would show that Trump's figleaf "national security" explanation for banning TikTok was an obvious lie.
 In other words, throughout this nonsense process, the President -- who at one point also  demanded a finder's fee for any deal -- made it clear that this was about his own whims. He was forcing a sale, and he was going to pick a winner -- and the winner had to be someone he liked.
 That doesn't seem like the kind of free market people appreciate. It seems like the very worst of a corrupt crony capitalist system.
 The WSJ piece details how being corrupt this whole process was. It was all about stroking Trump's ego, and lining his campaign's bank account:
 Sequoia Capital used its own connections to push the administration to allow TikTok to continue operating in the U.S., say people familiar with the discussions. Doug Leone, the firm’s global managing partner who took a lead role lobbying on TikTok’s behalf, has donated tens of thousands of dollars to Republican candidates this election season along with his wife, including to the president’s re-election campaign. Mr. Leone also held a reception at his Silicon Valley home for Secretary of State Mike Pompeo in January.
 Ms. Catz’s relationship to Mr. Trump could give Oracle a leg up in getting the deal approved, according to people in contact with the White House. Oracle has worked for decades with the U.S. government, including multiple contracts with the national-security establishment.
 Once again, this was nothing but performative nonsense from the President that ends in his own supporters lining their pockets. None of it seems to have anything to do with actual national security. It's a joke.
 Thank you for reading this Techdirt post. With so many things competing for everyone’s attention these days, we really appreciate you giving us your time. We work hard every day to put quality content out there for our community.
 Techdirt is one of the few remaining truly independent media outlets. We do not have a giant corporation behind us, and we rely heavily on our community to support us, in an age when advertisers are increasingly uninterested in sponsoring small, independent sites — especially a site like ours that is unwilling to pull punches in its reporting and analysis.
 While other websites have resorted to paywalls, registration requirements, and increasingly annoying/intrusive advertising, we have always kept Techdirt open and available to anyone. But in order to continue doing so,  we need your support. We offer a variety of ways for our readers to support us, from direct donations to special subscriptions and cool merchandise — and every little bit helps. Thank you.

昨天，对于公共卫生专家来说，有好消息，也有坏消息。在特朗普总统任命的美国卫生与公众服务部(Department Of Health And Human Services)公关主任越来越精神错乱之后，他和他任命的一名关键官员已经离职-一人离职两个月，一人永久离职。但是，任何认为科学可能会重新成为美国卫生政策的主要驱动力的希望都是短暂的。当天早些时候，疾控中心负责人罗伯特·雷德菲尔德(Robert Redfield)和其他卫生与公共服务官员在参议院小组作证。到了晚上，总统本人打电话给他自己的疾控中心主任，从口罩的使用到疫苗供应的时间表，他在所有问题上都搞错了。
当天结束时，雷德菲尔德在推特上发表声明，在含糊其辞和似乎支持特朗普观点之间取得平衡。
特朗普政府(它希望国家不顾医疗后果恢复正常运作)和公共卫生官员(他们实际上想要保护公众的健康)之间一直存在着紧张的背景。但最近有几件事使这些紧张关系公开化，从上周披露政治任命人员试图干预疾控中心职业科学家的报告开始。这个问题似乎得到了有利于疾控中心的解决，因为卫生与公众服务部(Department of Health and Human Services，HHS)的一位关键管理人物迈克尔·卡普托(Michael Caputo)在制作了一段视频后休了两个月的病假，他在视频中谈到了疾控中心科学家的武装起义和阴谋。
虽然卡普托的缺席是暂时的，但他最有问题的员工之一保罗·亚历山大(Paul Alexander)却被永久解雇了。亚历山大并没有悄悄离开，他告诉加拿大的“环球邮报”，疾控中心的科学家正在制作伪科学报告。
这些人都没有我的技能，亚历山大接着写道，我判断这是否是废话。这是在亚历山大向美国国立卫生研究院(National Institutes Of Health)知名官员安东尼·福奇(Anthony Fuci)抱怨内部电子邮件泄露后，亚历山大(错误地)告诉福奇博士，儿童不能传播SARS-CoV-2病毒。
所有这些似乎都会平息问题，并使HHS官员更有可能更好地传达准确的健康信息。但事实证明，缓刑是极其暂时的。
就在人事变动被报道的同时，几名HHS官员(包括疾控中心主任罗伯特·雷德菲尔德)正在参议院拨款委员会小组面前作证。虽然他们被问到了广泛的问题，但其中一些问题与循证公共卫生政策和有效疫苗的可能性有关。而且，考虑到特朗普政府对循证政策的做法，正如可能预期的那样，这些官员经常与他们的老板发生矛盾。
国会民主党人经常抓住机会盘问为特朗普政府工作的公共卫生专家。特朗普政府显然一直在散布错误信息，做出与我们的公共卫生知识背道而驰的政策决定。通过政府自己的专家的证词来强调这些矛盾，为民主党人提供了一个获得政治积分的黄金机会。但它也通过突出最好的公共卫生信息来服务于公共卫生目的。
虽然不可能确定这两个人中哪一个是民主党人质疑的主要动机，但听证会突显了特朗普政府和公共卫生专家存在分歧的一些关键问题。在某种程度上，那些作证的人尽其所能地躲避。当雷德菲尔德被问及特朗普淡化疫情严重性的决定时，他回答说，他不会对总统的决定发表评论，但会说疾控中心没有人建议特朗普这么做。
当人们提到戴面具时，类似的事情也发生了。卫生和公众服务部助理部长布雷特·吉罗尔表示，他不会对总统频繁拒绝使用口罩一事发表评论，但他确实表示，口罩是一种绝对关键的工具。他的助理国务卿同事罗伯特·卡德莱克(Robert Kadlec)简单地说，特朗普是一个可以行使自己权利的人。
但雷德菲尔德在促进公共健康方面工作最努力。他举起自己的口罩告诉委员会，这些是我们拥有的最强大的公共卫生工具。他接着重复了早先的声明，即广泛使用口罩6到12周可以控制住大流行。他作证说，我们有明确的科学证据表明它们有效，他说，与我接种COVID疫苗相比，它们保护我免受COVID的保护更有保证。他的推理是，所有的疫苗都不能以可测量的速度引发免疫反应，而所有的口罩至少提供了一定程度的保护。
当话题转移到疫苗上时，雷德菲尔德也注入了大量的现实。特朗普政府已经适当地开始规划，一旦疫苗通过安全性和有效性试验，就可以广泛分发。但是，目前的规划要求在11月总统选举之前开始配送，这一事实引发了人们的担忧，即这一时机可能是出于政治动机，而不是安全动机。而且，在最初的发行和广泛的供应之间显然会有很大的差距。
雷德菲尔德认为是时候让公众了解一下这一差距的大小了。他说，最初的供应时间将在11月至12月，但只会提供给医护人员和老年人等高优先级人群。大多数美国公众可能不得不等到2021年第三季度。
这种有益的现实主义剂量并没有持续太久。当天晚些时候，特朗普举行了新闻发布会，记者们很快就找到了他早先的声明与他自己的健康专家当天早些时候所说的话之间的差距。特朗普基本上是试图收回雷德菲尔德的言论。
当被问及使用口罩的问题时，特朗普说，首先，它并不比疫苗更有效，在接着说雷德菲尔德真的不知道他在说什么：我相信，如果你问他，他可能会说他不理解这个问题。尽管雷德菲尔德并没有被要求比较疫苗和口罩--他这么做只是为了强调使用口罩的重要性。(编者按：雷德菲尔德并没有被要求比较疫苗和口罩的效果)，但他接着说，雷德菲尔德真的不知道他在说什么：我相信，如果你问他，他可能会说他不明白这个问题。尽管雷德菲尔德并没有被要求比较疫苗和口罩--他这么做只是为了强调口罩使用的重要性。后来，特朗普说他给雷德菲尔德打了电话，据报道雷德菲尔德告诉他，我想我回答错了这个问题。
但特朗普并不满足于简单地解决口罩和疫苗的相对价值问题；他接着对口罩的使用不屑一顾，他说，我认为口罩有很多问题，并指出人们倾向于触摸口罩。后来，在没有回答为什么他不更频繁地使用口罩以身作则的问题后，特朗普直接反驳了雷德菲尔德关于口罩使用的科学证据的证词，他说，口罩可能会有帮助，我希望它会有帮助，我认为它可能会有帮助，但再一次，口罩是喜忧参半的。
疫苗分配问题最终完全被搞糊涂了。特朗普一度请来了他的一名健康顾问斯科特·阿特拉斯(Scott Atlas)(他有一些相当可疑的想法)来谈论这些计划。阿特拉斯计划的时间线与雷德菲尔德计划的大致相似。分销将在FDA批准后的一天内开始，具体日期目前尚不清楚。假设10月中旬获得批准，该计划将在1月底之前对最初的高危人群进行广泛的疫苗接种。在2021年第一季度末之前应该有足够的剂量可用，导致在第二季度末之前在整个美国人口中广泛接种疫苗。总体而言，与雷德菲尔德的证词相比，这只是轻微的加速。
当然，特朗普自己也一再表示，雷德菲尔德不知道他在说什么--字面意思。我的印象是，他没有意识到他说了自己可能会说的话，特朗普告诉媒体。在几点上，他暗示雷德菲尔德只是无法获得有关分销计划的信息，尽管疾控中心正在制作这些计划。在谈到雷德菲尔德的证词时，特朗普一度表示，这是不正确的信息。
特朗普还花时间表示，民主党人担心疫苗可能会因为政治原因而仓促接种，这实际上只是表明他们不想在选举前接种有效的疫苗。特朗普说，只有当他们意识到我们很可能在某个非常重要的日期(即11月3日)之前接种疫苗时，这种现象才会发生。一旦民主党人听到这一消息，他们就开始--仅仅是为了向你展示他们的意图有多坏--开始猛烈抨击疫苗。
特朗普还经常散布关于检测SARS-CoV-2的效用的错误信息，声称如果我们的检测次数减少，我们的病例就会减少。这是无稽之谈，HHS工作人员在作证时正确地确认了这一点。但特朗普在记者会上再次回到这一立场。特朗普表示，如果我们不进行测试，我们就不会有病例。你就不会有案子了。其他国家，他们不检测，他们没有病例。
与证词无关的是，特朗普还花了一些时间抨击另外一点公共卫生建议：通过限制人与人之间的互动来控制大流行。特朗普建议，如果我们拿出民主党运营的州的统计数据，美国可怕的流行病数据会看起来更好。之后，特朗普随机开始抱怨社会疏远政策，他说，开放自己的州非常重要。特朗普提到了民主党运营的三个摇摆州(密歇根州、北卡罗来纳州和宾夕法尼亚州)，他说，他们的限制对人们的伤害比疾病本身更大，然后恳求这些州让你们的人民拥有自由，这与司法部长比尔·巴尔(Bill Barr；)早些时候的抱怨如出一辙。在此之前，特朗普表示，他们的限制比疾病本身对人们的伤害更大，然后恳求这些州让你们的人民拥有自由，这与司法部长比尔·巴尔(Bill Barr；)早些时候的抱怨如出一辙。
特朗普再次表示，任何限制都是政治阴谋，而不是政府听从公共卫生建议。他们出于政治原因关闭了它，他说。它将于11月4日开业--它将在选举后立即开业。但他们想让人们..。他们希望我们的数据尽可能糟糕。问题是我们的数字太好了。
在新闻发布会接近尾声时，近乎妄想的坚持认为，当我们有最多的COVID病例和死亡人数时，美国的数字是好的。就在那时，一名记者问道，当特朗普与疾病控制与预防中心(CDC)负责人发生矛盾时，我们怎么能在流感大流行问题上相信他。“因为我们做了伟大的工作，”特朗普回答说。我们在新冠肺炎身上做了一件了不起的事，他们喜欢这样称呼它。
title: 白宫-疾控中心紧张局势爆发，特朗普与其领导层发生矛盾
title_en: White House-CDC tensions explode as Trump contradicts its leadership
